channel.title: Request for a Community Guides Forum Category
channel.description:
So I really would like to write some guides here on the forum that may be useful to the community but there is no such category. Although I understand guides could have harmful, wrong info, or AI slop so if you do add the category, you could require vetting by moderators or add a community notes to them.
Best Regards,
Q

item.title: Request for a Community Guides Forum Category
item.dc:creator: anon92632958
item.description:
Noted I will start some in the wiki not in forums thank you.

Read full topic
item.source: Request for a Community Guides Forum Category

item.title: Request for a Community Guides Forum Category
item.dc:creator: Patrick
item.description:
FranklyFlawless:

Moderators do not have the resources to vet them.

Exactly.

And if a user account is compromised, malicious edits can be made. Hard to review in the forums as the forum software isn’t designed for documentation/code edit review.

FranklyFlawless:

There has been a recent suggestion on the Qubes OS Forum about displaying a “last checked/edited by” quote footnote below a guide:

Re-inventing MediaWiki or similar documentation management software.

Read full topic
item.source: Request for a Community Guides Forum Category

item.title: Request for a Community Guides Forum Category
item.dc:creator: FranklyFlawless
item.description:
anon92632958:

you could require vetting by moderators or add a community notes to them.

Moderators do not have the resources to vet them. There has been a recent suggestion on the Qubes OS Forum about displaying a “last checked/edited by” quote footnote below a guide:

Qubes OS Forum – 21 Jan 26

Display "last checked by" and "last edited by" on Community guides?

Feedback Forum Feedback

Discourse allows to set up an automatic “last checked by” and “last edited by” field at the end of the documentation topic. I’m not really convinced, but it could be a way to remove the High Quality Guides category? (See High quality guides - #34...

Reading time: 1 mins 🕑 Likes: 15 ❤

However, so far, there has been no formal acknowledgement by the admin about it.

Read full topic
item.source: Request for a Community Guides Forum Category

item.title: Request for a Community Guides Forum Category
item.dc:creator: Patrick
item.description:
I don’t like documentation in forums. It belongs into the wiki to allow for collaborative editing. Related:

  • Wiki is the Primary Source of Information vs Forums
  • Security Guide Limitations

Also somewhat applicable:

Kicksecure – 6 Jan 26

First-Time Source Code Contributor Policy

Guidelines and expectations for first-time contributors to ensure a smooth and respectful development experience.

I.e. I suggest with small wiki edits first and build up from there.

Improve the Documentation / Edit the Kicksecure Wiki

Sample community maintained wiki chapter:
Anonymous Web Third-Party Hosting Providers

List of Hosting Providers That Accept Cryptocurrencies as Payment - General Tor and Anonymity Talk - Whonix Forum
p

Read full topic
item.source: Request for a Community Guides Forum Category

So I really would like to write some guides here on the forum that may be useful to the community but there is no such category. Best Regards,

Q
